item.title: FedEx chooses partnerships over proprietary tech for its automation strategy
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Szkutak
item.description: FedEx recently announced a partnership with Berkshire Gray as the company works with external players to develop its automation tech.

item.title: Nomadic raises $8.4 million to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company turns footage from robots into structured, searchable datasets with a deep learning model.

item.title: Health data giant CareCloud says hackers accessed patients’ medical records
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: CareCloud, a major provider of medical records storage, said hackers accessed one of its repositories of patient data earlier in March. It provides technology for more than 45,000 providers covering millions of patients.

item.title: Rivian spinoff Also will build autonomous delivery vehicles for DoorDash
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: DoorDash joins Greenoaks Capital in another $200 million funding round for Also, bringing its total funding to more than $500 million.

item.title: Roku launches a standalone app for Howdy, its $2.99 streaming service
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: With this new app, Roku says subscribers can access Howdy's library of content on the go.

item.title: Meta launches two new Ray-Ban glasses designed for prescription wearers
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Meta says these glasses are the most comfortable ones it has ever designed, as they're made for all-day comfort.

item.title: The company behind ClassPass and Mindbody just got a lot bigger with a $7.5B merger
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: The merger is a sign that the fitness industry is continuing to move toward consolidation to compete at a larger scale. Recent moves include MyFitnessPal acquiring Cal AI, an AI calorie counting app, and Strava buying two apps: cycling app The Breakaway and running app Runna.

item.title: Exclusive: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early stage AI startups
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Runway is launching a $10 million fund and startup program to back companies building with its AI video models, as it pushes toward interactive, real-time “video intelligence” applications.

item.title: Waymo starts robotaxi services at San Antonio International Airport
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: It's the fourth major airport Waymo is serving, adding to Phoenix Sky Harbor, San Francisco, and San Jose International airports.

item.title: Speechify’s Windows app uses local models for transcription and dictation
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Speechify just launched a native Windows app that employs locally stored models to enable dictation and transcription across apps.

item.title: With its new app store, Ring bets on AI to go beyond home security
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Ring's app store will allow the company to target broader use cases beyond security, like elder care or business needs.

item.title: Google is now letting users in the US change their Gmail address
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Users will be able to change their username only once every 12 months. Plus, they won't be able to delete their new email address for that period of time.

item.title: Airbnb is introducing a private car pick-up service
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Airbnb is partnering with transportation company Welcome Pickups to let users book a private car service during their trip.

item.title: The Silicon Valley congressional race is getting ugly
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The primary isn’t until early June, but the CA-17 race between five-term incumbent Ro Khanna and tech founder Ethan Agarwal is already getting nasty. Agarwal entered the race in March, backed by a roster of prominent tech billionaires largely in response to Khanna’s public support for a proposed California ballot measure that would impose a […]

item.title: Allbirds is selling for $39 million. It raised nearly 10 times that amount in its IPO.
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: The collapse of the brand, which was originally venture-backed and that went public in 2021, has been well-documented.

item.title: 15% of Americans say they’d be willing to work for an AI boss, according to new poll
item.dc:creator: Graham Starr
item.description: According to a Quinnipiac University poll, 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to have a job where their direct supervisor was an AI program that assigned tasks and set schedules.

item.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: LiteLLM had obtained two security compliance certifications via Delve and fell victim to some horrific credential-stealing malware last week.

item.title: Former Coatue partner raises huge $65M seed for enterprise AI agent startup
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A few things turned investors' heads and drew them to participate in such a big round out of the gate.

item.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: AI adoption is rising in the U.S., but trust remains low, with most Americans concerned about transparency, regulation, and the technology’s broader societal impact, according to a new Quinnipiac poll.

item.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026 and how to put your best application forward
item.dc:creator: Isabelle Johannessen
item.description: Here's what we're looking for in your Startup Battlefield 200 application and how to make sure your application reflects it.
